title: scolymocephalus coccineus
intro: Scolymocephalus coccineus, also known as the red calotropis, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. It is a type of flowering plant in the milkweed family, known for its bright red and yellow flowers. Scolymocephalus coccineus has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant can be used to prepare herbal remedies such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are believed to provide various health benefits including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing.
